channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Stardust trapped in Antarctic ice reveals tens of thousands of years of Solar System’s past - The Conversation
item.description:
1. Stardust trapped in Antarctic ice reveals tens of thousands of years of Solar System’s past  The Conversation
  2. Earth is flying through ancient supernova debris and scientists found the evidence in Antarctic ice  ScienceDaily
  3. The Ice Core That Caught Earth Traveling Through a Cloud of Supernova Dust  Indian Defence Review
  4. Stardust Trapped in Antarctic Ice Reveals Earth's Journey Through The Cosmos  ScienceAlert
  5. Path of the solar system through the Local Interstellar Cloud. The cloud’s profile is preserved as an interstellar fingerprint in Antarctic ice.  EurekAlert!

item.title: Hubble Discovers The Largest Known Planet Factory Unlike Anything Seen Before 1,000 Light-Years Away - The Daily Galaxy
item.description:
1. Hubble Discovers The Largest Known Planet Factory Unlike Anything Seen Before 1,000 Light-Years Away  The Daily Galaxy
  2. NASA’s Hubble reveals a giant chaotic planet nursery unlike anything seen before  ScienceDaily
  3. The Hubble Imaged The Largest-Yet Planet-Forming Disk  Orbital Today

item.title: Old Space Junk From the 1960s Just Revealed a Hidden Effect of the Sun on Earth’s Orbit - The Daily Galaxy
item.description:
1. Old Space Junk From the 1960s Just Revealed a Hidden Effect of the Sun on Earth’s Orbit  The Daily Galaxy
  2. Solar activity can influence how fast space junk loses orbit  Science News
  3. Solar activity makes space junk crash to Earth faster  Space
  4. Nature’s giant vacuum cleaner: Scientists reveal how the Sun is secretly cleaning Earth’s space junk  The Times of India
  5. Earth’s Congested Orbit: Policy Stagnation Behind the Proliferation of Space Debris  The Politic

item.title: Scientists Discover Neanderthals Were Using Teeth as Weapons, Just Not Their Own - Indian Defence Review
item.description:
1. Scientists Discover Neanderthals Were Using Teeth as Weapons, Just Not Their Own  Indian Defence Review
  2. Neanderthals used rhinoceros teeth as tools for making stone implements, study finds  Archaeology News Online Magazine
  3. News - Neanderthals May Have Used Rhino Teeth as Tools  Archaeology Magazine
  4. Modern experiments suggest rhino teeth may have been part of Neanderthal toolkits  Phys.org
  5. Neandertals used rhinoceros teeth as tools  Science News

item.title: The Milky Way ate a galaxy called Loki, and scientists think they found its bones - Live Science
item.description:
1. The Milky Way ate a galaxy called Loki, and scientists think they found its bones  Live Science
  2. Scientists Find Possible Dwarf Galaxy Inside Milky Way  GK Today
  3. Scientists Find Dwarf Galaxy That May Have Been Absorbed By Milky Way Billions Of Years Ago  NDTV
  4. Astronomers Say Something Massive Is Lurking Inside the Milky Way  VICE
  5. Astronomers identify 20 stars as remnants of Loki, a dwarf galaxy consumed by the Milky Way  starlust.org
